topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The doorbell rang, a jarring intrusion in the quiet afternoon. I froze mid-pour, the lukewarm tea sloshing precariously in the mug. Peeking through the peephole, my breath hitched. There, standing on the stoop, was a delivery person holding a medium-sized, plain brown box. My heart began a frantic drumming against my ribs. It was addressed to the former resident, Mrs. Henderson, who had moved out three weeks ago. A cold knot tightened in my stomach. I fumbled for the door, my hand slick with sudden perspiration. What if it was something… unexpected? I took a shaky breath, forcing my legs to move toward the entrance, every muscle tense as I approached the threshold.

[story 2]

I found the package tucked behind the overgrown lilac bush, the cardboard slightly damp. As I picked it up, the weight felt oddly substantial, and my fingers immediately started trembling. It was clearly addressed to the unit above mine. A wave of apprehension washed over me, making my palms feel clammy. I retreated quickly, backing away from the bush, trying to slow my rapid breathing. I walked back to my apartment, my eyes darting toward the window, half-expecting someone to be watching. The silence of the hallway seemed amplified, every creak of the floorboards sounding like a deliberate step. I set the box down on the kitchen counter, unable to bring myself to open it immediately.

[story 3]

The mail slot rattled, and I pulled the package out. It was heavier than anticipated, and as I examined the faded handwriting on the label, a distinct flutter started low in my chest. I kept glancing around the apartment, as if expecting an unseen presence to materialize from the shadows. My jaw tightened, and I had to consciously force my shoulders to relax, though the tension remained coiled tight in my neck. I carried the parcel into the living room, moving with an exaggerated slowness. Every rustle of fabric sounded deafening. I finally placed it on the coffee table, my gaze fixed on the tape sealing the box, waiting for some signal, some warning.
